text:
Once upon a quiet Tuesday afternoon in the sleepy town of Willow Creek, Mrs. Jenkins, the town's unofficial gossip, had just finished her daily meal of dry toast and tea at her cozy little cottage by the river.

"I tell ya," she began, her voice filled with the timbre of days spent behind her dusty rug. "I just saw Sammy, that rat-faced kid. Lyle told me he dropped by after school. No doubt he's up to his little tricks again."

Mrs. Jenkins took a sip from her tea, squinting at the grey sky. "He always does. It's the end of March, and we've got snowbanks taller than his ego. Still, Sammy's got a talent for finding trouble where there isn't any."

Just then, Nick Baxter, the son of Mr. Baxter, owner of Baxter's General Store, strolled in. He was a boy of seventeen, chatty as ever, and was always filled with rumors about the town
